title: Adapt a sequential explanatory design to AI feedback and student revision

research_logic: Estimate heterogeneous outcome patterns in a quantitative first strand.
Purposefully select follow-up participants from contrasting quantitative profiles.
Use interviews to explain how participants produced the observed patterns.
Integrate strands through a joint display and meta-inference.
strengths: The second strand is selected from a concrete quantitative puzzle.
Sampling connects the two strands rather than treating them as parallel projects.
The joint display makes convergence and divergence visible.
context_bound_elements: The original outcome is directly observed rather than rubric-scored.
The original participants use a standardized intervention.
The original sample is large enough for stable subgroup estimation.

user_project.topic: How AI-generated feedback relates to the quality of student revisions and why students respond differently.
available_materials: Access to a subset of students for interviews.
constraints: Students use different AI systems and prompts.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Use quantitative profiles to select contrasting follow-up participants.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Define revision-response profiles before interview sampling.
Record AI system, prompt, feedback type, and usage pattern as exposure heterogeneity.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Interpret subgroup differences from a large sample.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Use cautious profile description and uncertainty rather than unstable subgroup effect claims.

required_claim_boundaries: The integrated study may explain observed revision-response patterns in the studied courses.
Each component of the final claim identifies its quantitative, qualitative, or integrated warrant.
forbidden_inferences: AI feedback causes revision improvement without an identification strategy.
Interview accounts validate every quantitative association.
Parallel presentation of two strands counts as integration.
